preamble: The voiced palatal approximant /j/ can undergo a process of fortition, strengthening its articulation to become a palatalized voiced alveolar plosive /dʲ/. This transformation involves an increase in the degree of stricture, moving from an approximant to a stop, while maintaining its palatalized quality.
phoneticEffects: Fortition, increase in stricture, approximant to plosive, palatalization maintained

examples.note: Sino-Vietnamese *j became dʲ in the 17th century.
Word-initial strengthening of /j/ to /dʲ/ in the Upper Yeniseian dialect.
Earlier *j shows free variation between [j] and [dʲ].

sources: How was the /j/ initial in Middle Chinese borrowed into Vietnamese?. (2015, August 23). Linguistics Stack Exchange.
